metadata.summary: Canonical layered architecture for Spring Boot 3.x: @RestController → @Service → JpaRepository. CRUD endpoints with Bean Validation, MapStruct, @Transactional, and Pageable lists.

content.title: Java Spring Boot Backend
content.summary: Canonical layered architecture for Spring Boot 3.x: `@RestController` → `@Service` → `JpaRepository`. Covers CRUD endpoints with Bean Validation, MapStruct DTO mapping, `@Transactional` write methods, and `Pageable` list endpoints. This is the starter Spring Boot pattern; for richer search, Specifications, and ProblemDetail see related methodologies.

why.p: The layered pattern separates HTTP concerns (controller), business logic (service), and persistence (repository). Without explicit DTO mapping, controllers leak entity internals (Hibernate proxies, lazy fields). Without `@Transactional` on writes, multi-step operations rely on auto-commit per JDBC call, leaving inconsistent state on partial failure.

list.item: New Spring Boot 3.x service with standard CRUD endpoints.
Adding endpoints with Bean Validation, MapStruct DTO mapping, and paginated list queries.
Refactoring code that mixes controller/service/repository concerns or returns entity types to clients.
Wiring BCrypt `PasswordEncoder` and paginated `Pageable` defaults in the service layer.
WebFlux/reactive stack — blocking JPA, `@Transactional`, and `ResponseEntity` semantics differ.
Hexagonal/Clean Architecture with explicit ports/adapters — layering on top doubles up indirection.
CQRS or event-sourced systems — service+JPA hides the command/query split.
Quarkus, Micronaut, or serverless — annotations differ; pattern translates conceptually but not literally.

section.name: layered-architecture
section.p: The three-layer pattern (controller → service → repository) cleanly separates concerns and allows independent testing of each layer.

rule.statement: Controllers MUST be thin: receive HTTP request, call service, return `ResponseEntity`. Services MUST contain business logic and MUST annotate write methods with `@Transactional` (non-write methods default to `@Transactional(readOnly=true)` at class level). Repositories MUST be `JpaRepository` subinterfaces with custom JPQL queries only when needed; use `Specification` for complex filters. Controllers MUST NEVER return entity types; always return DTOs.
rule.rationale: Layering isolates database schema changes from HTTP API contracts. `@Transactional` on writes ensures multi-step operations succeed-or-rollback atomically. Testability is maximized: slice tests can mock service, unit tests can mock repository. DTOs prevent lazy-loading exceptions and Hibernate proxy leaks.

example.title: Controller layer (thin)
example.note: Controller delegates all logic to service. Returns DTO (UserResponse), never entity. HTTP semantics only.

example.title: Service layer (business logic with @Transactional)
example.note: Class-level `readOnly=true`; individual `@Transactional` on writes. Constructor injection. Uses mapper for DTO conversion.

example.title: Repository layer with custom JPQL
example.note: `LEFT JOIN FETCH` prevents N+1 queries. Custom JPQL for complex filters avoids entity load overhead.

example.title: DTO types (records)
example.note: Records are immutable and concise. Bean Validation on request DTOs only.

antipattern.description: Controller returns entity type directly: `public User findById(Long id)` without DTO.
antipattern.reason: Lazy-loading fields serialize to null. Hibernate proxies leak into JSON. Schema changes break API contracts. Always use DTOs.

antipattern.description: Service method missing `@Transactional` on multi-step writes (save → update → delete sequence).
antipattern.reason: Each JDBC call auto-commits. Partial failure leaves inconsistent state. Partial rollback impossible.

antipattern.description: Calling another `@Transactional` method on `this` instead of injecting the bean.
antipattern.reason: Spring proxies don't wrap self-invocation. The called method runs without a transaction, silently.

section.name: entity-and-mapping
section.p: Entity design and DTO mapping are critical to avoid lazy-loading bugs and schema leaks.

rule.statement: Entities MUST use constructor injection via `@RequiredArgsConstructor` (Lombok). Entities MUST have an `@Id` field of type `Long` and MUST use `@CreationTimestamp` / `@UpdateTimestamp` for audit columns. Entities MUST NOT be returned from controllers; always map to DTO via MapStruct. Lazy-loaded fields (e.g., `@OneToMany`) MUST be fetched explicitly via `LEFT JOIN FETCH` or converted in the service layer.
rule.rationale: Audit columns track data mutations for compliance and debugging. MapStruct generates efficient mapping code at compile time. Explicit fetch prevents N+1 and LazyInitializationException. Controlling what's serialized prevents information leaks.

example.title: Entity with Lombok and timestamps
example.note: Timestamps, unique email, lazy roles. Lombok reduces boilerplate. Builder for test fixtures.

example.title: MapStruct mapper (code-generated)
example.note: MapStruct generates code at compile time. Field ignores ensure immutable fields stay constant. No manual converter logic.

antipattern.description: Lazy-loading a collection in the controller and then serializing it: `return user.getRoles().stream()...`
antipattern.reason: LazyInitializationException if the session is closed after the service call. Move the fetch into the repository query or service layer.

section.name: pagination-and-queries
section.p: List endpoints must paginate to prevent unbounded table scans. Custom queries guard against N+1.

rule.statement: Every list endpoint MUST accept `Pageable` as a parameter and MUST return `Page<DTO>`. Queries returning multiple rows MUST use `LEFT JOIN FETCH` or separate queries to prevent N+1. Missing database indexes on frequently queried columns MUST be flagged in code review.
rule.rationale: Pagination prevents scanning multi-million-row tables in a single query, avoiding OOM and timeout. `Pageable` leverages Spring Data's limit/offset and page size defaults. N+1 queries cause latency cascades in prod; `JOIN FETCH` solves it at the repository layer.

example.title: Paginated list endpoint
example.note: `Pageable` with sensible defaults. Callers use `?page=0&size=20` to navigate. Service returns `Page<UserResponse>` with metadata (totalElements, totalPages, hasNext).

antipattern.description: Endpoint returning `List<DTO>` from `findAll()` on a 10M-row table.
antipattern.reason: OOM, timeout, and query lock contention. Prod incident.

antipattern.description: Loading a user, then looping over roles inside the controller and issuing a separate query per role.
antipattern.reason: N+1 queries. 1 user + 100 roles = 101 queries. Explicit `LEFT JOIN FETCH` solves this in 1 query.

section.name: validation-and-error-handling
section.p: Bean Validation on request DTOs + global error advice in `@RestControllerAdvice` provides clean API error responses.

rule.statement: Request DTOs MUST use Bean Validation annotations (`@NotBlank`, `@Email`, `@Size`, etc.). Controllers MUST NOT manually check validation; rely on `@Valid` to trigger `MethodArgumentNotValidException`. Services MUST throw domain exceptions (e.g., `ResourceNotFoundException`, `ConflictException`). A global `@RestControllerAdvice` MUST map exceptions to `ResponseEntity` with structured error payloads (status, message, field errors).
rule.rationale: Bean Validation is declarative and reusable. Global advice ensures consistent error format across endpoints. Exceptions propagate intent without explicit try-catch boilerplate. Field errors in the response help clients pinpoint validation failures.

example.title: Request DTO with validation
example.note: Validation rules are declarative. `@Valid` in the controller triggers validation automatically.

example.title: Global exception advice
example.note: Centralized error handling. Consistent response format. Validation errors include field names.

antipattern.description: Service returning `null` or an empty Optional without throwing an exception.
antipattern.reason: Controller must null-check every result. Inconsistent error messages. Global advice cannot handle it uniformly.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Drive feature work as SDD tasks: migration → entity → repository → service (with `@Transactional` defaults) → MapStruct mapper → controller → integration test.

Reviewer agent must run `./mvnw verify`, parse Hibernate SQL logs for N+1, and check that controllers never expose entities. When the project graduates beyond simple CRUD (search, complex validation, ProblemDetail), switch to richer pattern methodologies.
Recommended subagents:

list.item: `faion-sdd-executor-agent` — task runner, executes Maven/Gradle build per task.
`feature-executor` — feature-level orchestration with quality gates.
General reviewer subagent — flag missing `@Transactional`, entity leaks, missing `Pageable` on list endpoints.

section.p: Prompt pattern for planning:
section.prompt: Feature `<name>`: Flyway migration, entity (Long id, audited via `@CreationTimestamp`/`@UpdateTimestamp`), `JpaRepository`, service with `@Transactional(readOnly=true)` default and `@Transactional` writes, MapStruct mapper, `@RestController` with `@Valid` request bodies, integration test using Testcontainers + Postgres.
section.p: Prompt pattern for review:
section.prompt: Run `./mvnw test`. Check controllers do not return entity types, all writes are `@Transactional`, all list endpoints accept `Pageable`. Flag findings.

section.name: tools-and-services
section.p: Standard tooling for Spring Boot development.
Build: `mvnw` / `gradlew` (shipped), `spring init` CLI via sdkman.
Format: Spotless (spotless.dev) with Google Java style.
Lint: Checkstyle (checkstyle.org), Error Prone + NullAway (errorprone.info).
Testing: Testcontainers (testcontainers.org) for real DB integration tests; ArchUnit (archunit.org) for architecture validation.
Migrations: `flyway` / `liquibase` CLI for applying migrations from CI.
Lombok plugin (projectlombok.org) for reducing boilerplate. MapStruct (mapstruct.org) for DTO ↔ entity mapping.
CI platforms: GitHub Actions / GitLab CI with standard JDK + cache templates.
Observability: Sentry / Bugsnag (sentry-spring-boot-starter), OpenTelemetry Java agent (zero-code instrumentation), Datadog / New Relic / Dynatrace (auto-attach to JVM).
Quality gates: SonarQube / SonarCloud for code smell and security scanning.
Bootstrap: Spring Initializr API (https://start.spring.io) for new projects.

list.item: Default `@Service` to `@Transactional(readOnly = true)` at class level; override `@Transactional` per write method.
Constructor injection via `@RequiredArgsConstructor`; never field injection.
DTOs are records (Java 17+); never expose entities from controllers or include Hibernate proxies in JSON.
Always paginate list endpoints; reject unbounded `findAll()` on tables that may grow.
Use Bean Validation on request DTOs; map `MethodArgumentNotValidException` in `@RestControllerAdvice`.
Hash passwords with `PasswordEncoder` (BCrypt) inside the service layer; never store cleartext, never accept the hash from the client.
Run integration tests against a real DB via Testcontainers; H2 in-memory hides PostgreSQL-specific behaviour.
Agents return entities from controllers, then add `@JsonIgnore` to fields trying to fix Lazy loading — DTOs are the fix, not annotations.
LLMs forget `@Transactional` on multi-step writes; partial commits look fine in tests but corrupt prod data.
Self-invocation: calling another `@Transactional` method on `this` does not start a tx. Reviewer must catch this.
Agents miss the MapStruct annotation processor in `pom.xml` and the mapper class fails to generate — looks like a mysterious "interface cannot be instantiated" error.
`findAll(Pageable)` is sometimes converted to `findAll()` "for simplicity"; require pagination on every list endpoint.
Generated tests mock `JpaRepository` with `@MockBean`, hiding query bugs; require Testcontainers for repository tests.
Spring Boot 3 + `jakarta.*` vs Spring Boot 2 + `javax.*` namespace mixing is a frequent LLM error; pin Spring Boot version in the prompt.

template.purpose: Pre-commit gate: compile + Spotless + block controllers returning entity types
